sourceMovie.title: Dark Waters
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Dark Waters (2019) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Todd Haynes's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Bird Box
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dark Waters clicked for you, Bird Box (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Illusionist
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dark Waters clicked for you, The Illusionist (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dark Waters?
faq.answer: Try Bird Box, The Illusionist, Changing Lanes—each shares DNA with Dark Waters while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dark Waters?
faq.answer: Dark Waters (2019) is best described as Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dark Waters worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
